note: Locations of the violence_peace Reach, reached from the Gralloch's edge (violence-ward) or over the marches. Same asymmetry principle: violence_peace dominant, but the secondary axis rolls varied per node. Poles expressed as poleIntensity (independent), per the corrected model. Locations use full schema. Authored by Aevi (PO).

locations.name: The Redline
locations.descriptionSeed: The violent frontier of the Marches — but honed, not feral. A warrior culture whose entire society is disciplined conflict: duels settle law, standing is won on the training-ground, and the Edge is taught to children as literacy. What rides with the violence here is BODY — the perfected physical, movement as language. Terrifying to a stranger, and rigorously fair once you learn the forms. Not the Gralloch's lawlessness; the opposite — violence with rules so strict they're almost peace.
locations.encounterFlavor: Honed conflict as culture — a formal duel with witnesses, a child besting an adult by the forms, a challenge you can accept or honorably decline, a warrior reading your stance before your words.
questSeeds: You're challenged on arrival by custom — how you handle a duel you didn't want (fight, decline, turn it to a Contest) sets your whole standing in the Marches
A Marcher champion is being pushed toward a war they know is unjust; they want an outsider to give them a way to refuse without dishonor
The Edge is being taught to someone who shouldn't have it — a person whose rage the discipline can't hold; the Marchers are afraid of what they made

locations.name: The Stillhold
locations.descriptionSeed: A walled city of absolute peace — no violence in living memory, no weapons, no raised voices. Beautiful, ordered, and subtly WRONG. What rides with the peace here is FALSEHOOD: the stillness rests on something no one is allowed to say, a load-bearing untruth so old it has become the air. The peace is real and the peace is a prison, and both are true. The counter-image of the Redline — and arguably the more dangerous place, because its foreclosure wears serenity.
locations.encounterFlavor: Serene wrongness — a kindness with a warning under it, a question that makes everyone go quiet, a peace so total it reads as held breath, the slow realization that no one here is allowed to leave the subject alone.
questSeeds: You're welcomed warmly and gently steered away from one question; following it is the whole arc, and the Stillhold will resist you with kindness, not force
Someone inside wants OUT — not of the city, of the silence; helping them speak the unsayable thing might free them or shatter the peace of thousands
The truth the Stillhold is built on is discoverable, and learning it forces the framework's exact question: is a peace built on a buried injustice worth keeping

locations.name: The Marchward
locations.descriptionSeed: The habitable border-town between the Redline and the Stillhold — where the violence-pole and the peace-pole both run moderate and TRUTH runs high, because a place that touches both extremes learns to speak plainly or die. The natural base for exploring the Reach: warriors and peace-keepers drink at the same tables here and mean it, uneasily. A both-poles-moderate node — the March itself, made a town.
locations.encounterFlavor: Uneasy honest coexistence — a Redline duelist and a Stillhold exile at the same hearth, a truth spoken because pretense is too expensive here, a mediation that could go either way, a place holding its own contradiction in the open.
questSeeds: The Marchward survives by neutrality and someone is trying to break it — pull it toward the Redline or the Stillhold; holding the balance is the work
A Redliner and a Stillholder have fallen in love across the wall and both cultures are appalled; the Marchward is where they've fled
A truth from the Stillhold has leaked here and the Marchward has to decide whether to carry it to the world or bury it again

asymmetry_note: Three violence_peace nodes, varied second poles: the Redline rides BODY (honed martial culture), the Stillhold rides FALSEHOOD (peace on a buried lie — the surprising, damning secondary), the Marchward is both-moderate + TRUTH (the honest border). Same axis, three worlds: disciplined war, false peace, honest neutrality.
